nativeRange.habitat: Has been documented over mixed sand, rubble, and seaweed-covered bottoms near coral reefs, commonly in protected bays and lagoons or on deeper outer-reef slopes from roughly 1 to 100 m. Juveniles tend to associate with shallower seagrass and rubble zones in schools, while adults are typically solitary.

decorPreferences: deep fine sand bed for barbel foraging

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding mainly on benthic worms, small crustaceans (shrimps, crabs, krill), brittle stars, small molluscs, and heart urchins in the wild. In captivity, generally advised to offer a varied meaty diet of mysis, krill, chopped shrimp, squid, and clam, sinking to the substrate where the fish actively probes with its chin barbels. Has been reported to need frequent (3–5 small) feedings daily due to a high metabolism, especially when newly introduced; some individuals tend to refuse dry/pellet foods initially and may only accept live or frozen items.

avoidWith: small fish that fit in the mouth

summary: A Western Pacific reef-associated goatfish with a burgundy front half and yellow tail divided by a white band. Constantly sifts sand with chin barbels; generally considered reef-safe only with caution and best suited to large peaceful systems.
careNotes: Parupeneus barberinoides is a medium-sized Indo-West Pacific goatfish; adults are commonly reported around 25 cm with maxima close to 30 cm TL. Sources differ on minimum tank size — LiveAquaria lists 130 gallons (~492 L), Reefapp.net advises 700 L, and AquaticCommunity gives a substantially smaller 50 gal / 200 L figure; given the species' continuous bottom-sifting behaviour, eventual adult size, and need for open swimming room, the conservative 700 L (≈180 cm length) value is used here. Temperature sources also vary: FishBase reports a wild preferred range of 24.7–28.9 °C (mean 27.6 °C); LiveAquaria advises 72–78 °F (≈22.2–25.6 °C); AquaticCommunity lists 22–26 °C. The 23–27 °C range used here is an average of these. A deep fine sand bed is generally advised so the fish can use its chin barbels to forage naturally; expect the substrate to be stirred and the water to cloud after feeding. Has been widely reported as a capable jumper, so a tightly fitting lid or covered overflow is strongly advised. Generally peaceful toward similarly sized tankmates but is a documented predator on small mobile invertebrates (ornamental shrimps, peanut worms, small crabs and gastropods, brittle stars), so it is commonly considered reef-safe only with caution and unsuitable for tanks containing prized small inverts. Adults tend to be solitary in the wild while juveniles form schools; aquarists generally keep this species singly. Wild-caught specimens have been reported to commonly arrive picky about feeding — some individuals refuse prepared foods initially and need live or fresh meaty items until acclimated.
breedingNotes: Not known to be bred in captivity; no published aquarium spawning protocols were located in the consulted sources.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Designated primary. Used for care level (Difficult → advanced), temperament (peaceful), reef safety (with caution), water parameters (pH 8.1–8.4, dKH 8–12, SG 1.020–1.025, 72–78 °F), minimum tank size (130 gal ≈ 492 L), origin (Fiji), and dietary frequency notes.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Mullidae, Mulliformes), maximum length (30 cm TL, common 20 cm), wild preferred temperature (24.7–28.9 °C, mean 27.6 °C), depth range (1–100 m), Western Pacific distribution (Moluccas/Philippines to Samoa, Ryukyus to New Caledonia/Tonga, Palau/Caroline/Marshall Is., W. Australia), benthic-invertebrate diet (worms, crustaceans, brittle stars, small molluscs, heart urchins), and adult-solitary / juvenile-schooling behaviour.

additional.name: Reefapp.net
additional.notes: Cross-checked larger minimum tank size (700 L), max size (~30 cm), temperament (docile, shy), reef-safety caveat (threat to some inverts), feeding frequency advice, jumping risk, and Western Pacific distribution.

additional.name: AquaticCommunity (Dash-and-dot Goatfish)
additional.notes: Cross-checked pH 8.1–8.4, salinity SG 1.020–1.025, temperature 22–26 °C, max size ~25 cm, non-reef-safe note, peaceful temperament, and dietary advice (often refuses pellets, needs frozen/live meaty items).
